summary: AWS Pinpoint to Amazon Connect Migration Testing Complete - All 18 backend tests passed (100%), frontend migration verified successfully
critical_code_review_comments: Migration properly implemented - status endpoint now returns 'connect' key instead of 'pinpoint'
All old Pinpoint routes correctly return 404 (completely removed)
All new Connect routes work correctly: instances, queues, contact-flows, hours-of-operation, users, routing-profiles
WorkMail functionality preserved and still works after migration
Frontend page correctly shows Amazon Connect tab instead of Pinpoint tab
Both services show 'Connected' status in the UI
context_for_next_testing_agent: AWS Pinpoint has been fully replaced with Amazon Connect. The route file is still named aws_workmail_pinpoint_routes.py but contains Connect logic. Frontend page at /aws-comms shows WorkMail and Amazon Connect tabs. Status endpoint returns 'connect' key (not 'pinpoint'). AWS account has 0 Connect instances so empty states are expected.

features_tested.Status Endpoint - Pinpoint Key Removed: PASSED - No 'pinpoint' key in status response
features_tested.Connect Instances List: PASSED - Returns 200 with instances array (empty expected)
features_tested.Connect Queues Validation: PASSED - Requires instance_id query param (422 without)
features_tested.Connect Contact Flows Validation: PASSED - Requires instance_id query param (422 without)
features_tested.Connect Hours of Operation Validation: PASSED - Requires instance_id query param (422 without)
features_tested.Connect Users Validation: PASSED - Requires instance_id query param (422 without)
features_tested.Connect Routing Profiles Validation: PASSED - Requires instance_id query param (422 without)
features_tested.Frontend Status Cards: PASSED - WorkMail and Amazon Connect cards visible with 'Connected' badges
features_tested.Frontend Tabs: PASSED - WorkMail and Amazon Connect tabs present, no Pinpoint tab
features_tested.Frontend Connect Tab Content: PASSED - Shows Connect Instances section with empty state
features_tested.Frontend WorkMail Tab Content: PASSED - Shows Organizations section, still works

expected_behavior_notes.Empty Connect Resources: AWS account has 0 Connect instances, so empty lists/states are expected
expected_behavior_notes.Connected Status: Both WorkMail and Connect services show 'Connected' indicating proper AWS SDK configuration
